html:
Exclusive to:  

The Science Machine is the tier 1 Science station. It costs 1 Gold Nugget, 4 Logs, and 4 Rocks to craft, and it is available from the start of the game. Like other Prototyper, standing near the Science Machine allows prototyping new recipes. Prototyping items for the first time will grant the player 15 Sanity. Once prototyped, items can be crafted without being near a Science Machine.

See also: Crafting 

Crafting Recipes

Downloadable Content

In the Shipwrecked DLC, Science Machine can be flooded causing it to malfunction and become unusable.

Tips

  • When Wickerbottom is near a Science Machine, it acts as an Alchemy Engine, as she is already able to craft tier 1 science items, thus making the Science Machine serve as a tier 2 Science station.
  • Science machines may be hammered after an alchemy engine has been built to save resources.
  • Wickerbottom should skip the Science machine when playing in DST to let others also use tier 2 science recipes.

Trivia

  • In the early Beta version of Don't Starve, the game used a "Research Points" system, instead of prototyping. When Wilson examines the Science Machine, he states that "It breaks down items into their scientific components", which is a leftover dialogue script from when the game used Research Points.
  • Its old crafting description was "Probe the depths of the universe!".
  • The main section of this machine bears a resemblance to the weighted storage cubes in Valve's Portal series.
  • The Science Machine appears at the top of the screen when the game "Autosaves".
  • There appears to be rope turning the wheels on the machine, while no rope is needed to build it.

Gallery

  • The animation played when the player is near a Science Machine.
  • A burnt Science Machine from the Reign of Giants DLC.
  • An idle Science Machine.
  • Science Machine as seen on the 2018 Valentine Cards.
  • Dont Starve research machines gif by Kelly [1]
  • In "Bachelor of Science" achivment
  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.

References

  1. ↑ Web archive of Kelly Graham's blog.

Exclusive to:
“It breaks items into their scientific components.”
–Wilson
“Even I dont know everything, yet.”
–Willow
“I am not sure how I feel about... science.”
–Wolfgang
“I will learn unspeakable things.”
–Wendy
“MOTHER?”
–WX-78
“It's a research station. I can learn new things with it.”
–Wickerbottom
“I don't trust all this science stuff.”
–Woodie
“I can't believe I forgot how to build those things.”
–Maxwell
“How interesting! Proximity affects its usability.”
–Wagstaff
“I prefer battle tö science.”
–Wigfrid
“It's like a science lab in here!”
–Webber
“I wish you were a do-all-the-work-for-me machine.”
–Walani
“A centre for learning.”
–Warly
“I heard 'bout this science stuff. 'Tis up 'n comin'.”
–Woodlegs
“WILBA AM SO SMART”
–Wilba
“Makes more things!”
–Wormwood
“Time to make some discoveries of the scientific variety!”
–Wheeler
“Rickety, but I can use it to build things.”
–Winona
“Mortals play such silly games.”
–Wortox
“Funny-hair man say "Science" come out of it?”
–Wurt
“I never knew science was such an important part of survival.”
–Walter
“I really want to know what makes it tick...”
–Wanda
The Science Machine is the tier 1 Science station. It costs 1 Gold Nugget, 4 Logs, and 4 Rocks to craft, and it is available from the start of the game. Like other Prototyper, standing near the Science Machine allows prototyping new recipes. Prototyping items for the first time will grant the player 15 Sanity. Once prototyped, items can be crafted without being near a Science Machine.
See also: Crafting

Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Shipwrecked DLC, Science Machine can be flooded causing it to malfunction and become unusable.
Tips[edit | edit source]
When Wickerbottom is near a Science Machine, it acts as an Alchemy Engine, as she is already able to craft tier 1 science items, thus making the Science Machine serve as a tier 2 Science station.
Science machines may be hammered after an alchemy engine has been built to save resources.
Wickerbottom should skip the Science machine when playing in DST to let others also use tier 2 science recipes.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
In the early Beta version of Don't Starve, the game used a "Research Points" system, instead of prototyping. When Wilson examines the Science Machine, he states that "It breaks down items into their scientific components", which is a leftover dialogue script from when the game used Research Points.
Its old crafting description was "Probe the depths of the universe!".
The main section of this machine bears a resemblance to the weighted storage cubes in Valve's Portal series.
The Science Machine appears at the top of the screen when the game "Autosaves".
There appears to be rope turning the wheels on the machine, while no rope is needed to build it.

}} =={{Pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content== In the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, Science Machine can be [[Flooding|flooded]] causing it to malfunction and become unusable. == {{pic|32|Prototype}} Tips == * When [[Wickerbottom]] is near a Science Machine, it acts as an [[Alchemy Engine]], as she is already able to craft tier 1 science items, thus making the Science Machine serve as a tier 2 Science station. * Science machines may be hammered after an alchemy engine has been built to save resources. * Wickerbottom should skip the Science machine when playing in DST to let others also use tier 2 science recipes. == {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * In the [[Don't Starve/Version History|early Beta]] version of ''[[Don't Starve]]'', the game used a "[[Research points list|Research Points]]" system, instead of prototyping. When [[Wilson]] examines the Science Machine, he states that "It breaks down items into their scientific components", which is a leftover dialogue script from when the game used Research Points. * Its old crafting description was "''Probe the depths of the universe!''". * The main section of this machine bears a resemblance to the weighted storage cubes in [[Wikipedia:Valve Corporation|Valve]]'s ''[[Wikipedia:Portal (series)|Portal]]'' series. * The Science Machine appears at the top of the screen when the game "Autosaves". * There appears to be rope turning the wheels on the machine, while no rope is needed to build it. =={{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery== Science Machine.gif|The animation played when the player is near a Science Machine.
